resources.string: Global default state; when off, the input bar chip can still toggle it temporarily
The following features are experimental and may be unstable or affect your experience
Attempt to require MOOD block output even if the model does not support it
Auto-summarize very long conversations (enabled by default since v1.55)
Everything you fill in here is injected into the AI\'s system prompt so it truly knows you. All fields are optional.
The AI will call itself by this name
The AI will address you by this name
A paragraph introducing yourself, giving the AI an overall sense of who you are
Highest priority; gives the AI a holistic view of you
e.g. BSc Computer Science / MSc Financial Engineering
e.g. Python, Machine Learning, Product Design
The AI will proactively avoid these topics
Covers overseas, domestic official, and relay providers — just enter your API Key after selecting
Manually enter baseUrl, API Key, and model list
Preset message templates with variable substitution, insert with one tap
Auto-inject mode-specific prompts when switching modes (creative/coding/roleplay, etc.)
View/enable/disable tools the LLM can call (file I/O, HTTP, etc.)
When off, conversations no longer inject long-term memory and background summarization stops
When enabled, the main assistant can delegate tasks to team members
Tap below to create a team
No assistants available. Please create one in the Assistant page first.

Muse is an AI smart assistant app. You can think of it as a friend who knows everything — ask it questions anytime, have it help you write, translate, summarize, and more.\n\nIt needs an internet connection and an AI model to work, so you\'ll need to do a quick setup before first use.
What to do for first use?

Don't worry, just three steps to get started:

1. Get an API Key for an AI model (like a pass — see Chapter 2)
2. Add this Key in Settings
3. Go back to the chat page, type a message, and press send

It's that simple!
How to start your first conversation

1. Open the app and go to the chat page
2. Tap the input box at the bottom of the screen (where it says "Send message...")
3. Type your question, e.g. "Hello"
4. Tap the send button on the right (paper plane icon)
5. Wait for the AI to reply — text will appear word by word; this is called "streaming output"
Why do I need to configure a model?

Muse itself does not contain AI — it needs to connect to an external AI service. Just like a phone needs a SIM card to make calls, Muse needs an \"API Key\" to connect to AI.\n\nAn API Key is a password made of letters and numbers, obtained from an AI provider.
Where to apply for an API Key?

Here are some commonly used AI providers (in no particular order):

• DeepSeek — domestic, affordable, great results
 Website: platform.deepseek.com

• Zhipu AI — domestic, free quota available
 Website: open.bigmodel.cn

• Alibaba Tongyi Qianwen — domestic, free quota available
 Website: dashscope.aliyun.com

• Moonshot Kimi — domestic, strong long-context capability
 Website: platform.moonshot.cn

• OpenAI (ChatGPT) — international, best results but requires overseas payment
 Website: platform.openai.com

Beginners should start with DeepSeek or Zhipu. After registering, find the "API Keys" page in the dashboard, click "Create new Key", and copy the code.
How to add a provider in Muse

1. Tap the bottom-right or top-left to enter "Settings"
2. Find "Models & Services" or "Providers"
3. Tap "Add Provider"
4. Select the provider type (e.g. OpenAI Compatible)
5. Fill in:
 - Name: anything you like, e.g. "My DeepSeek"
 - API Key: paste the code you just copied
 - Endpoint: for DeepSeek, enter https://api.deepseek.com
6. Tap Save
7. Add the model you want to use in the model list (e.g. deepseek-chat)
How to select the current model
On the chat page, the model name is displayed near the top or input bar. Tap it to bring up the model selection list, then choose the one you want.
How to start a new conversation
On the chat list page (sidebar or conversation list), tap the \"New Chat\" button (usually a plus icon). A new conversation is blank and won\'t be affected by previous conversations.
Open the sidebar from the chat page (tap the top-left menu or swipe from the left edge) to see all conversation history. Tap any one to switch to it.
In the conversation history list, long-press the conversation you want to delete. A menu will pop up — select \"Delete\". Note that deletion cannot be undone.
On the chat page or conversation list, find the search icon (magnifying glass), enter a keyword, and all historical messages containing that word will be shown.

When the AI replies, text doesn\'t appear all at once — it comes out word by word, like typing. This is called \"streaming output\". The benefit is you don\'t have to wait long to see the answer; the downside is it requires a stable network. If the network is poor, it may stall — just wait a moment.

Assistant roles are like putting different \"hats\" on the AI. For example, you can create a \"Translator\" role that focuses on translation, or a \"Writing Assistant\" role that excels at writing articles.\n\nFind the \"Assistant\" entry in Settings or the sidebar, tap to create, fill in the name and system prompt (tell it how it should behave), and it\'s done.

There\'s a globe icon above the input box. Tap it to highlight it and enable web search. The AI will search the internet for the latest information before answering — great for current events and news. Tap again to turn it off when done to save data.
How to use deep thinking mode

There\'s a brain icon above the input box. Tap to highlight and enable it. When enabled, the AI will \"think\" before answering — suitable for math problems, logical reasoning, and other complex questions. Not needed for regular chat.

Tap the plus icon on the left of the input box, select \"Image\", and pick a photo from your gallery to send. The AI can recognize image content. Note: you need to select a model that supports \"vision\" (e.g. GPT-4o, Gemini, etc.).
Tap the microphone icon on the right of the input box, speak, and release to automatically convert to text. You need to configure a speech recognition (ASR) service to use this feature.

Tap the plus icon on the left of the input box and select \"Prompt Template\" to see a list of preset templates (e.g. write email, translate, summarize). Tap a template to auto-fill the input box — just modify the specifics and send.

If you\'re unsatisfied with one of the AI\'s replies and want to restart from that message, long-press that message and select \"Fork Conversation\". A new conversation will be created, copying all previous content, and you can take the conversation in a different direction from there.
Settings → Appearance → Theme Mode, choose \"Follow System\" (auto-follows phone settings), \"Light\", or \"Dark\". Dark mode is easier on the eyes at night.
Settings → Appearance → Text Size, choose Small/Medium/Large/Extra Large. Medium is the default size.
Settings → Appearance → Language, choose \"Follow System\", \"Chinese\", or \"English\". The interface changes immediately after switching.

Settings → Data & Backup lets you configure cloud backup (WebDAV or S3). Once configured, chat history will be automatically backed up to the cloud — no worries about losing data when switching phones.

Settings → Data Import supports importing from RikkaHub or Kelivo. After selecting the source, follow the prompts to export a backup file from that app, then select it here. Import content includes provider configs, assistants, and conversation records.

On the Assistant Manager page, long-press an assistant and select \"Export Character Card\" to generate a file you can share with friends. Friends can import it in Muse to use an assistant with the same configuration.

Most common causes:
1. Wrong API Key — check in Settings
2. No network — try a different network
3. Insufficient balance — top up on the provider's website
4. Wrong endpoint — check against the official documentation

1. Switch to a faster model (smaller models are usually faster)
2. Check your network connection
3. Turn off "Deep Thinking" mode (thinking takes time)
4. If using OpenAI in China, you need a proxy

1. Configure \"Tool Model Routing\" in Settings to use cheaper small models for tool calls\n2. Turn off unnecessary web search\n3. Choose domestic models (DeepSeek/Zhipu, etc.) — much cheaper than OpenAI

Settings → Security & Sharing may have relevant options. Or clear Muse\'s data in your phone\'s \"App Management\" system settings. Note: this deletes all conversations and configs and cannot be undone.
